title: Passive cooling apparatus for cooling of immersion fluid and power conversion equipment including a shape-memory alloy heat engine and related systems
abstract: A passive fluid cooling system is provided including an immersion cooling tank including a volume of fluid; and a shape memory alloy (SMA) heat engine, the SMA heat engine including a plurality of wheels and an SMA wire coupled to the plurality of wheels. A portion of the SMA wire is in contact with the volume of fluid and transitions from a relaxed state to a tightened state when a temperature of the volume of fluid exceeds a transformation temperature of the SMA wire. The tightening of the SMA wire causes the plurality of wheels to rotate and induce motion in the volume of fluid and causes the volume of fluid to decrease in temperature.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A passive fluid cooling system, comprising: an immersion cooling tank including a volume of fluid; and a shape memory alloy (SMA) heat engine, the SMA heat engine including a plurality of wheels and an SMA wire coupled to the plurality of wheels, wherein a portion of the SMA wire is in contact with the volume of fluid and transitions from a relaxed state to a tightened state when a temperature of the volume of fluid exceeds a transformation temperature of the SMA wire; and wherein tightening of the SMA wire causes the plurality of wheels to rotate and induce motion in the volume of fluid and causes the volume of fluid to decrease in temperature. 2. The system of Claim 1 , wherein the plurality of wheels remain in motion until the temperature of the volume of fluid is reduced to below the transformation temperature of the SMA wire. 3. The system of Claim 1, wherein the plurality of wheels comprises first and second pullies, wherein the first pulley is larger than the second pulley and wherein the second pulley is immersed in the volume of fluid. 4. The system of Claim 1, wherein the plurality of wheels comprise first and second blades, wherein the first blade is larger than the second blade and wherein the second blade is immersed in the volume of fluid. 5. The system of Claim 1, wherein the SMA comprises Nitinol. 6. The system of Claim 1 , wherein fluid velocity inside the immersion cooling tank and air speed over an external radiator caused by the SMA heat engine increases heat transfer capability of the passive cooling system. 7. The system of Claim 1, wherein the SMA heat engine draws power from a temperature differential inside and outside of the immersion cooling tank. 8. The system of Claim 1, wherein the SMA heat engine induces flow rates in the immersion cooling tank without addition of external power. 9. The system of Claim 1, wherein the fluid is thermally conductive and electrically insulating. 10. The system of Claim 1 , wherein the immersion cooling tank further includes electronic components positioned in the immersion cooling tank and wherein the cooled volume of fluid cools the electronic components positioned in the immersion cooling tank. 11. A shape memory alloy (SMA) heat engine for use with a passive cooling system, the SMA heat engine comprising: a plurality of wheels and an SMA wire coupled to the plurality of wheels, wherein a portion of the SMA wire is in contact with a volume of fluid and transitions from a relaxed state to a tightened state when a temperature of the volume of fluid exceeds a transformation temperature of the SMA wire; and wherein tightening of the SMA wire causes the plurality of wheels to rotate and induce motion in the volume of fluid and causes the volume of fluid to decrease in temperature. 12. The engine of Claim 11, wherein the plurality of wheels remain in motion until the temperature of the volume of fluid is reduced to below the transformation temperature of the SMA wire. 13. The engine of Claim 11, wherein the plurality of wheels comprises first and second pullies, wherein the first pulley is larger than the second pulley and wherein the second pulley is immersed in the volume of fluid. 14. The engine of Claim 11, wherein the plurality of wheels comprise first and second blades, wherein the first blade is larger than the second blade and wherein the second blade is immersed in the volume of fluid. 15. The engine of Claim 11 , wherein the SMA comprises Nitinol. 16. The engine of Claim 11 , wherein fluid velocity inside an immersion cooling tank and air speed over an external radiator caused by the SMA heat engine increases heat transfer capability of the passive cooling system. 17. The engine of Claim 11, wherein the SMA heat engine draws power from a temperature differential inside and outside of an immersion cooling tank. 18. The engine of Claim 11 , wherein the SMA heat engine induces flow rates in the immersion cooling tank without addition of external power. 19. The engine of Claim 11, wherein the fluid is thermally conductive and electrically insulating. 20. The engine of Claim 11 , wherein the immersion cooling tank further includes electronic components positioned in an immersion cooling tank and wherein the cooled volume of fluid cools the electronic components positioned in the immersion cooling tank.
